events.title: George Michael would have been 63
events.blurb: The iconic Wham! frontman and solo artist George Michael would have celebrated his 63rd birthday.

angles.name: Remembering George Michael
angles.copy: Remembering the incredible George Michael, who would have turned 63 tomorrow. His music continues to define generations and soundtrack our lives. A true legend.

events.title: Michael Jackson's passing remembered
events.blurb: It marks 17 years since the King of Pop, Michael Jackson, passed away.

angles.name: MJ Anniversary
angles.copy: Seventeen years ago, the world lost Michael Jackson, a true icon whose music and moves changed everything. His legacy lives on.

events.title: Ringo Starr turns 86
events.blurb: The legendary Beatles drummer Ringo Starr celebrates his 86th birthday.

angles.name: Happy Birthday Ringo
angles.copy: Happy 86th birthday to the one and only Ringo Starr! From The Beatles to his solo work, his beat is unmistakable. A true legend of rock and roll.

events.title: Brian May turns 79
events.blurb: Queen's iconic guitarist Brian May celebrates his 79th birthday.

angles.name: Happy Birthday Brian May
angles.copy: Wishing a very happy 79th birthday to the legendary Brian May! His guitar riffs are pure magic and have shaped rock music for decades. Long live the Queen legend!

events.title: Mick Jagger turns 83
events.blurb: The Rolling Stones' charismatic frontman Mick Jagger celebrates his 83rd birthday.

angles.name: Happy Birthday Mick Jagger
angles.copy: Can you believe Mick Jagger is turning 83? The energy, the moves, the voice – he's still got it. Happy birthday to the ultimate rock and roll icon!

events.title: MTV celebrates 45 years
events.blurb: Music Television (MTV) launched 45 years ago, with The Buggles' 'Video Killed The Radio Star' being its first ever broadcast.

angles.name: MTV Anniversary
angles.copy: Forty-five years ago today, MTV launched and changed music forever, kicking off with The Buggles' 'Video Killed The Radio Star'. The irony isn't lost on us!

events.title: Oasis's 'Definitely Maybe' turns 32
events.blurb: Oasis released their seminal debut album 'Definitely Maybe' 32 years ago.

angles.name: Definitely Maybe Anniversary
angles.copy: Thirty-two years ago, Oasis dropped 'Definitely Maybe' and instantly became one of Britain's biggest bands. An absolute game-changer. Pure 90s gold.

events.title: Woodstock Festival 57th Anniversary
events.blurb: The iconic Woodstock Music & Art Fair, a pivotal moment in music history, took place 57 years ago.

angles.name: Woodstock Anniversary
angles.copy: Fifty-seven years ago, half a million people gathered for Woodstock, a festival that defined a generation. Peace, love, and incredible music. Imagine being there!

events.title: Remembering Elvis Presley
events.blurb: It marks 49 years since the world lost the King of Rock and Roll, Elvis Presley.

angles.name: Elvis Anniversary
angles.copy: Forty-nine years ago, we lost the King, Elvis Presley. His voice, his style, his impact – truly unparalleled. The legend lives on.

events.title: Remembering Tupac Shakur
events.blurb: It marks 30 years since rap icon Tupac Shakur was tragically shot in Las Vegas.

angles.name: Tupac Anniversary
angles.copy: Thirty years ago, the music world lost a giant. Remembering Tupac Shakur, whose powerful lyrics and undeniable talent left an indelible mark. His legacy endures.

events.title: Remembering Jimi Hendrix
events.blurb: It marks 56 years since the legendary guitarist Jimi Hendrix passed away.

angles.name: Jimi Hendrix Anniversary
angles.copy: Fifty-six years since the world lost Jimi Hendrix, a true guitar god who redefined what was possible with six strings. His sound is timeless.

events.title: The Beatles' 'Abbey Road' turns 57
events.blurb: The Beatles released their iconic album 'Abbey Road' 57 years ago.

angles.name: Abbey Road Anniversary
angles.copy: Fifty-seven years ago, The Beatles walked across that zebra crossing and released 'Abbey Road'. An absolute masterpiece, and that cover is still iconic.

events.title: Chrissie Hynde turns 75
events.blurb: The Pretenders' formidable frontwoman Chrissie Hynde celebrates her 75th birthday.

angles.name: Happy Birthday Chrissie Hynde
angles.copy: Happy 75th birthday to the incredible Chrissie Hynde! A true rock icon with a voice that's instantly recognisable. Still rocking hard!

events.title: Liam Gallagher turns 54
events.blurb: The unmistakable voice of Oasis and solo artist Liam Gallagher celebrates his 54th birthday.

angles.name: Happy Birthday Liam Gallagher
angles.copy: Happy 54th birthday to the one and only Liam Gallagher! Still bringing that swagger and those tunes. As you were!

events.title: John Lennon's 'Imagine' turns 55
events.blurb: John Lennon's iconic song 'Imagine', from the album of the same name, was released 55 years ago in 1971.

angles.name: Imagine Anniversary
angles.copy: Fifty-five years ago, John Lennon released 'Imagine', a timeless anthem for peace and unity. Its message resonates as strongly today as ever. A true masterpiece.

events.title: Original iPod turns 25
events.blurb: Apple released its revolutionary first-generation iPod 25 years ago, changing how we listen to music forever.

angles.name: iPod Anniversary
angles.copy: Can you believe it's 25 years since Apple launched the original iPod? It put '1,000 songs in your pocket' and completely revolutionised music listening. A true game-changer!

events.title: Happy James Bond Day!
events.blurb: October 5th is celebrated annually as James Bond Day, marking the release date of the first 007 film, 'Dr. No', in 1962.

angles.name: James Bond Day
angles.copy: Happy James Bond Day! Today we celebrate 007, the iconic spy who's given us decades of thrilling films, incredible gadgets, and unforgettable theme tunes. Shaken, not stirred.

events.title: John Lennon would have been 86
events.blurb: The legendary Beatles co-founder John Lennon would have celebrated his 86th birthday.

angles.name: Remembering John Lennon
angles.copy: Remembering the visionary John Lennon, who would have turned 86 today. His songwriting, his activism, his spirit – forever influential. Imagine all the people...
